title: Star Wars: The Visual Dictionary
content:

'''''Star Wars: The Visual Dictionary''''' is a reference book written by [[David West Reynolds (real person)|David West Reynolds]] and released by [[Dorling Kindersley|DK Publishing]] on [[September 19]], [[1998]]. It is the first book in the ''[[Star Wars Visual Dictionaries]]'' series of ''[[Star Wars]]'' reference books, covering the [[original trilogy]] of ''Star Wars'' movies—[[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']], [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'']] and [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']]—with photographs and descriptions of elements from those movies. ''The Visual Dictionary'' was also included in the [[1999]] ''Star Wars Classic Limited Edition Gift Pack''.

It was later followed by ''Visual Dictionaries'' for each of the [[prequel trilogy]] films, and the four books were combined with new content and republished in [[2006]] as ''[[Star Wars: The Complete Visual Dictionary]]''.
